titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Sandra Berrigan Papers
titlestmt.author: Elise Riley

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:28

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, writings, printed material, realia, and other papers by or relating to Sandra Berrigan.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 8 (correspondence): Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Correspondence, 1961-2024. II. Materials Related to Dear Sandy, Hello, 2005-2011. III. Realia, circa 1988-2018.

bioghist.head: Sandra Berrigan (1942- )
bioghist.p: Sandra "Sandy" Berrigan née Alper was born in 1942 in Detroit, Michigan. At age 13, her family moved to Miami, Florida where her father, Louis Alper, was a practicing physician.

Berrigan attended Sophie Newcomb College in New Orleans where she met Dick Gallup, a student at Tulane University. In February 1962, Gallup introduced Sandy to his friend visiting from New York, Ted Berrigan (born Edmund Joseph Michael Berrigan Jr.). The two quickly fell in love and, within a week, Sandy and Ted got married in Houston, Texas. The newlyweds agreed to visit Sandy's parents in Miami and a few days later Louis and Dorothy Alper obtained a court order to commit Sandy to the Jackson Memorial Hospital's mental ward. Sandy was held in the hospital from February to May 1962, during which time she and Ted exchanged numerous letters full of poetry and philosophical discussion. In early May, Sandy was allowed to visit the public library on good behavior. Ted was waiting for her there and together they fled Miami eventually landing in New York City.

In June 1962, the Alpers again had Sandy committed first at Bellevue's mental ward and then later at the Euphrasian Girls' Home. She was released on July 26, 1962. Sandy and Ted remained married for the next seven years, had two children, and then separated in 1969. During this time Sandy met other poets and writers in Ted's circle including Ron Padgett, Tom Veitch, and Joe Brainard. This is also the period when Ted is working on his most famous book, The Sonnets (1964). Sandy eventually moved California, raised her two children, and continued to correspond with the poetry community she had become a part of. She wrote and collaborated on poetry of her own and in 2010 she co-edited -- with Ron Padgett -- the collection of the letters she and Ted sent to each other in 1962 into the book, Dear Sandy, Hello .

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Sandra Berrigan papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Archival Description Unit Processing Manual.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related materials: Ron Padgett papers (YCAL MSS 721); Maureen Owen collection of Greenwich Village poetry (YCAL MSS 218).
Associated materials: Ted Berrigan papers (Columbia Rare Book & Manuscript Library).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Sandra Berrigan Papers consists of her personal and literary correspondence, as well as realia related to Sandra Berrigan's life and work as a writer and poet.

The correspondence captures Berrigan's many decades long friendships with poets and creatives in and around the second generation of the New York School of poets including Ron Padgett, Dick Gallup, Anne Waldman, Joe Brainard, and Maureen Owen. Among the correspondence are Berrigan's original letters that she exchanged with her former husband and poet, Ted Berrigan. These letters were written in 1962 while she was held at the Jackson Memorial Hospital mental ward at the behest of her parents during the beginning of the Berrigan's marriage. Also included in this collection are drafts of the book Dear Sandy, Hello and three poetry quilts Sandra created by incorporating pieces of poetry sent to her by friends.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Sandra Berrigan Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into four sub-series: Incoming, Outgoing, Third Party, and Poetry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence sent to and from Sandra Berrigan, as well as third party correspondence related to Berrigan's marriage and family. Included in this series is the original correspondence between Sandra and her former husband, Ted Berrigan, written while Sandra was held in the Jackson Memorial Hosiptal mental ward from March to May of 1962.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 8: Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 8: Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence received by Sandra Berrigan. Included in the materials are decades long correspondence with close friends including Ron Padgett, Maureen Owen, Anne Waldman, and Carla Harryman. Also included in this sub-series is correspondence from Sandra's former husband, Ted Berrigan, some of which is featured in the published book of their mutual correspondence from the early days of their marriage, Dear Sandy, Hello .

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted until January 1, 2050. For further information consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by name of the recipient.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence sent by Sandra Berrigan. Included in this sub-series is correspondence she sent to her former husband, Ted Berrigan, some of which is featured in the published book of their mutual correspondence from the early days of their marriage, Dear Sandy, Hello .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by name of the sender.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence sent between third party individuals. Of note in this sub-series is correspondence sent by Sandra Berrigan's former husband, Ted Berrigan, and his mother, Margaret, to Sandra's parents while she was held in Jackson Memorial Hospital's mental ward.

did.unittitle: Berrigan, Margaret to Louis and Dorothy Alper
Berrigan, Ted to Louis and Dorothy Alper
Powers, Anthony C. to Ted Berrigan

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence wherein Sandra Berrigan collaborated with the sender on writing poetry.

did.unittitle: Abbott, Keith, Pat Nolan, Maureen Owen, and Michael Sawl regarding "Sky Renku"
Nolan, Pat regarding miscellaneous renga practice lines
Materials Related to Dear Sandy, Hello

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is arranged into two sub-series: Correspondence and Drafts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, reference material, and proofs related to the publication of the book, Dear Sandy, Hello . This book is the edited version of the correspondence exchanged between Ted and Sandy when Sandy was held in the Jackson Memorial Hospital's mental ward from February to May 1962.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged alphabetically by name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains correspondence related to the editing and publication of the book, Dear Sandy, Hello .

did.unittitle: Padgett, Ron, Maureen Owen and Carla Harryman

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this sub-series are arranged chronologically by date.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This sub-series contains reference materials and proofs related to the editing and publication of the book, Dear Sandy, Hello .

did.unittitle: Copies of early poems by Ted Berrigan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains three 'poetry quilts' made by Sandra Berrigan. Each quilt features poems written or typed onto pieces of cloth Berrigan provided to her close friends. Also included in this series is correspondence related to the creation of the poetry quilts.
